Join our ongoing conversation on slack with the link below.

name: Deb Haaland
subject: I'm running.
body:
I'm launching my campaign for governor of the great state of New Mexico. |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102299629/528725143/-847979181?refcode=WUEFH235_20250217_DHxELRS_ALL&sourceid=1055712&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOWMyZWY4Zi03YWVkLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IzEZf9nExCp62ErZNVp8OmUImu7QtitkXbcyYl6-Q3s=) My friends at EMILYs List said I could reach out with some exciting news, Peter:

Last week, I launched my campaign for governor of the great state of New Mexico — my home — to bring our state together and build a bright future for all who live here. The next day, EMILYs List endorsed me. I couldn't be more grateful for their support.

So I'm humbly asking: [**Will you join us by splitting a donation between my campaign and EMILYs List now?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102299632/528725147/-847979181?refcode=WUEFH235_20250217_DHxELRS_ALL&sourceid=1055712&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOWMyZWY4Zi03YWVkLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IzEZf9nExCp62ErZNVp8OmUImu7QtitkXbcyYl6-Q3s=) | [SPLIT A DONATION](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102299640/528725151/-847979181?refcode=WUEFH235_20250217_DHxELRS_ALL&sourceid=1055712&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOWMyZWY4Zi03YWVkLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IzEZf9nExCp62ErZNVp8OmUImu7QtitkXbcyYl6-Q3s=)
---
The amount you give doesn't matter. What matters is building a campaign with the support of real people. That's who I am and who I came from. That's who I am running to serve.

My roots in New Mexico run 35 generations deep. The Pueblo people were the first farmers in the high deserts of New Mexico, an agriculture tradition that still continues today.

I am here because of my grandfather, who worked for decades as a diesel mechanic on the railroad. Somehow he also kept a field — picking worms off the corn as it grew, like the generations before him.

I am here because of my grandmother, a survivor and a caretaker who woke up every day before the sun. She was the one who gave me my true love of cooking New Mexican cuisine. (She wouldn't let us in the kitchen while she cooked, but I'd perch myself outside and watch her through the window for hours.)

I am here because of my parents and their sacrifice. My mother, a Pueblo woman, served in the Navy. My father, a Marine, received a Silver Star for his service in Vietnam. He now lies in honor at Arlington.

I am here because of my beloved child, who I raised on my own. I started a small business to pay the bills, but it still wasn't always enough. We relied on food stamps to get on our feet. I sometimes had to decide between paying rent and buying groceries to feed our family. I will never forget how that felt or the kindness of those who helped us — strangers who became community. When I look at my Somah, I see perseverance, strength and hope.

These are my stories.

These are stories of New Mexico.

These days, our differences are blown so far out of proportion. We lose sight of a simple fact: we all live in the same state, together. All of us in New Mexico come from love, survival, and sacrifice. We all want to live good lives — and we want even more for our children.

The current president and his billionaire friends look at the lands and the people of this country and think: What can I exploit?

I am running to ask: If we come together in New Mexico, what good can we do for each other and for the next generation?

We have our work cut out for us. The cost of living is crushing our families. Our economy is growing, but we need to make sure everyone shares in our progress. Our families need more. Our farmers and ranchers need more. New Mexico's children need so much more. And we must be good stewards of the Earth, and preserve the rivers, mesas, farmlands, and forests whose beauty sustains our communities.

I am running to do all this and more. I am running to serve all New Mexicans and make sure we're leaving a better future for our children.
[**Will you join us with a donation, split between my campaign and EMILYs List?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/102299649/528725162/-847979181?refcode=WUEFH235_20250217_DHxELRS_ALL&sourceid=1055712&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOWMyZWY4Zi03YWVkLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IzEZf9nExCp62ErZNVp8OmUImu7QtitkXbcyYl6-Q3s=) I would be honored to have your support.

clean_body: I'm launching my campaign for governor of the great state of New Mexico. | = My friends at EMILYs List said I could reach out with some exciting news, Peter: Last week, I launched my campaign for governor of the great state of New Mexico — my home — to bring our state together and build a bright future for all who live here. The next day, EMILYs List endorsed me. I couldn't be more grateful for their support. So I'm humbly asking: = | = The amount you give doesn't matter. What matters is building a campaign with the support of real people. That's who I am and who I came from. That's who I am running to serve. My roots in New Mexico run 35 generations deep. The Pueblo people were the first farmers in the high deserts of New Mexico, an agriculture tradition that still continues today. I am here because of my grandfather, who worked for decades as a diesel mechanic on the railroad. Somehow he also kept a field — picking worms off the corn as it grew, like the generations before him. I am here because of my grandmother, a survivor and a caretaker who woke up every day before the sun. She was the one who gave me my true love of cooking New Mexican cuisine. (She wouldn't let us in the kitchen while she cooked, but I'd perch myself outside and watch her through the window for hours.) I am here because of my parents and their sacrifice. My mother, a Pueblo woman, served in the Navy. My father, a Marine, received a Silver Star for his service in Vietnam. He now lies in honor at Arlington. I am here because of my beloved child, who I raised on my own. I started a small business to pay the bills, but it still wasn't always enough. We relied on food stamps to get on our feet. I sometimes had to decide between paying rent and buying groceries to feed our family. I will never forget how that felt or the kindness of those who helped us — strangers who became community. When I look at my Somah, I see perseverance, strength and hope. These are my stories. These are stories of New Mexico. These days, our differences are blown so far out of proportion. We lose sight of a simple fact: we all live in the same state, together. All of us in New Mexico come from love, survival, and sacrifice. We all want to live good lives — and we want even more for our children. The current president and his billionaire friends look at the lands and the people of this country and think: What can I exploit? I am running to ask: If we come together in New Mexico, what good can we do for each other and for the next generation? We have our work cut out for us. The cost of living is crushing our families. Our economy is growing, but we need to make sure everyone shares in our progress. Our families need more. Our farmers and ranchers need more. New Mexico's children need so much more. And we must be good stewards of the Earth, and preserve the rivers, mesas, farmlands, and forests whose beauty sustains our communities. I am running to do all this and more. I am running to serve all New Mexicans and make sure we're leaving a better future for our children. = I would be honored to have your support. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Many thanks to EMILYs List for their early support. I hope you'll join us, Peter! Be fierce, Deb = = Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Inbox overcrowded? Sign up to get less email . We'd hate to see you go, but if you want to stop receiving all email, unsubscribe . Have a question? Feel free to drop us a line at mailto:information@emilyslist.org or simply reply to this email. Contributions or gifts to EMILYs List or endorsed candidates are not tax deductible. EMILYs List P.O. Box 96612 Washington, DC 20077 United States | ---|---

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Standing up for Virginians
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Governor
body:
Last week, I joined MSNBC to discuss Trump’s plan to fire federal workers and threaten the jobs of thousands of Virginians.

| Peter,

**Last week, I joined MSNBC to discuss Trump’s plan to fire federal workers and threaten the jobs of thousands of Virginians.**
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102299788/528725656/1993432096?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-02-17_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWEwZmMyMWQtN2JlZC1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Zp4RzfQFC6gSWCLXfSHwtgQIfCAePCjx-VkHYYvb6wE=)
Who are these workers? They’re the Social Security workers making sure that our parents and grandparents receive the benefits they’ve earned, the law enforcement officers working tirelessly to protect our country from terrorist threats, and so many others. They are moms and dads. They are neighbors, community leaders, and public servants who are woven into the fabric of what makes Virginia strong. And now, their jobs are at risk.

**And what has Virginia’s current leadership done—including our Governor and Lieutenant Governor (my opponent)?_They’ve fully backed this plan full stop._**

We deserve better. We deserve a Governor who will stand up for the people of Virginia, not cheer on the chaos and division that Trump is spreading.

That's why I’m running for Governor of Virginia. I will always stand with our hardworking Virginians. I’ll make sure that we protect the livelihoods of our federal workers and their families — and I will work to strengthen our economy by investing in the workforce that makes Virginia thrive.

[_**But I need your help. Can you contribute $10, $25, or $50 today to help me build a campaign that puts Virginians first?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102299789/528725657/1993432096?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-02-17_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWEwZmMyMWQtN2JlZC1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Zp4RzfQFC6gSWCLXfSHwtgQIfCAePCjx-VkHYYvb6wE=)

clean_body: Last week, I joined MSNBC to discuss Trump’s plan to fire federal workers and threaten the jobs of thousands of Virginians. | Peter, Last week, I joined MSNBC to discuss Trump’s plan to fire federal workers and threaten the jobs of thousands of Virginians. = Who are these workers? They’re the Social Security workers making sure that our parents and grandparents receive the benefits they’ve earned, the law enforcement officers working tirelessly to protect our country from terrorist threats, and so many others. They are moms and dads. They are neighbors, community leaders, and public servants who are woven into the fabric of what makes Virginia strong. And now, their jobs are at risk. And what has Virginia’s current leadership done—including our Governor and Lieutenant Governor (my opponent)?They’ve fully backed this plan full stop. We deserve better. We deserve a Governor who will stand up for the people of Virginia, not cheer on the chaos and division that Trump is spreading. That's why I’m running for Governor of Virginia. I will always stand with our hardworking Virginians. I’ll make sure that we protect the livelihoods of our federal workers and their families — and I will work to strengthen our economy by investing in the workforce that makes Virginia thrive. = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you, Abigail Spanberger * | | Paid for by Spanberger for Governor Spanberger for Governor P.O. Box 3121 Glen Allen, VA 23058 United States | = =

name: State Representative Ryan Chamberlin
subject: Special Session Newsletter from Rep. Ryan Chamberlin
body:
Special Session Newsletter from Rep. Ryan Chamberlin | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/repryanchamberlin/newsletter-from-rep-ryan-chamberlin-12724422?e=d1c1b9864f)
---
| |
---
| | **SPECIAL SESSION NEWSLETTER FROM REP. RYAN CHAMBERLIN**
---
|
---
| Friends and Neighbors,

As I promised in a previous newsletter, today I’m going to provide an update on special session. This past Thursday, alongside the leadership of President Donald Trump and Governor Ron DeSantis, my fellow legislators and I passed the strongest legislation in the country to combat illegal immigration.

Florida is standing with President Trump’s America-First agenda and is leading the way for the rest of the states. The legislation that we passed requires an automatic death penalty for illegal aliens who commit murder or rape a child, strengthens penalties to keep dangerous criminal illegals locked up, creates new state-level crimes for illegally entering and reentering Florida, eliminates in-state tuition for illegal aliens, and so much more.

In Florida, we are committed to holding the line on illegal immigration and keeping our communities safe. The last four years under the Biden-Harris administration weakened our country and put American citizens last. Thankfully, those days are behind us. We have now entered a new golden age here in America with Donald Trump back in the White House, and I’m incredibly proud to support his efforts to Make America Great Again.

Please see the graphic below for a closer look at Florida’s new toughest-in-the-nation illegal immigration enforcement laws.
---
|
---
|
---
| | Thank you for reading, and I look forward to sharing with you again soon.

Have a great week!

Best regards,

**Ryan Chamberlin**
******State Representative**
**District 24**
---
| | _Copyright © 2024 Ryan Chamberlin for State Representative, All rights reserved._

Paid by Ryan Chamberlin, Republican, for State Representative

Ryan Chamberlin for State Representative
526 E Park Ave
Tallahassee, FL 32301-2568

name: Team WFP
subject: Making airplanes more dangerous so Elon Musk can get richer
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
| | | Peter, The Musk-Trump rampage to break the federal government has been running full steam ahead. And all of us — across the country — are about to feel the impact. Over the weekend, roughly 3,400 workers in the US Forest Service (USFS) and 1,000 in the National Parks Service (NPS) were terminated1 — many of whom are tasked with wildfire prevention or rescuing stranded visitors. **And this morning, several hundred were let go from the Federal Aviation Association 2 — just 18 days after the deadliest commercial plane crash since 2009.** These are just a few of the more than 200,000 federal workers in every state who have been targeted for layoffs. And it’s only the beginning. [Elon Musk is selling our safety and security for his own personal tax breaks. Sign our petition today and tell Congress to kick Elon Musk out of our government, before he tears down even more vital safety services.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRImmimh3jp2d-3TZzmeeGfu-UTQdsDuya64KpuO8JCNfpJz62tZbS1uRaEs6xz5o92rDg2XhEAJvsJFbdUoEPfHeyhplR8xlGYyiPdqSrTFjOz2aUIv1O58mTQR6k1Q3SFJRISkVFjG9idUbYtR897hASeyybC4bNxJ19_fGABomj7Q-Ux4Yr7WUX67xaTKoevIYNb_Xjl6yLhDU8o5iBpQ9sFxmybYo3bPkji-yFacWKAOwmBJLWgSBraSgz4urKGO2TlHkKj6prm6cFJf9R-KDfUrF6VwQbJWVVgpmUoPjQYF3QOskt7dhRPIlB3Y-YOa1_fdrHAMjFbZBKmMiEx9Jr3uQeHlvAV4JfXKXGJAcQ/4e3/C_6HjgErSGS9G_eszVbRrg/h0/h001.YCrhRKfByFcKwa2TQn_KksrNxz96FEC2bSQgHylpv_c) Already signed? [Make a donation to WFP](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquqxZ_WMtWQ5nsYnjR236T9rsQK6hrV_0q7voEM_Bgdt_LTJ6b-R68ikaF4GtJSgT_0j1DsL9PfUpKVlitfAJf8K_tiXTYlKVakn8PWf2Fy7aCskKlqH-wH0GWiDSJhqdOxnpKY0gLbCX_t3Ud0JUiv60nzg09MtIgZHA5edD4FXFgPIm9eO4WQ4JF_grXsB74YU7J4UMDe8BtbqNoySRssn1YWkhlOj3vkzT3tcYT4EhXvUz7JsE4Qdkj1kFbIvi_ZOT6r_Q6LbNk5FmwvNOEXJ-petc9WvK56M2k7jkew7QhRTduFp7Lpb_ym3hbWS_jPsJd3Ls3xtVo2QUwCnMEj7t4cCyLeHily7WQ5n7i_Bg/4e3/C_6HjgErSGS9G_eszVbRrg/h1/h001.m5wgFnD-BBy1hwlHjER0VznkBAmrtN7XUJAgYyKMxf4) to help fuel our organizing. Or take a moment to [join the WFP Call Team](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxLu_Xi4kTg6U5gTalj1phaR5tri0M3AFXSmAG3t9m4fVYS1lDl8g0CcP1U3nAE4SLRk8y2CA2O9B-L-sjZvGhdz2DpkD-662xxb41_LGMlcxBkX8Jus_sfOBKRQ7vZ_HZZ656H7RNMXR2vB0kWm_yR1dgGfYeuG20sXRmIBcOlFqjtbt5gM8c74kmhaJ9qD_QFbynnRoZ9tuK8GD3cHPLOtKwRU0yB_2MukfDAfS87lnpt90DqAZS4XrLrWKJrtdu6gzorywL5Bmtnm5M9hJ2fQ/4e3/C_6HjgErSGS9G_eszVbRrg/h2/h001.j4IsFrMnWBBGQVVWfeYS1KqYlazfOtRfPR1pEblMWCo) to be notified as more accountability, rapid-response, and election priority phonebanks are posted. **[Sign the petition »](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRImmimh3jp2d-3TZzmeeGfu-UTQdsDuya64KpuO8JCNfpJz62tZbS1uRaEs6xz5o92rDg2XhEAJvsJFbdUoEPfHeyhplR8xlGYyiPdqSrTFjOz2aUIv1O58mTQR6k1Q3SFJRISkVFjG9idUbYtR897h5zXRMdg2WBUfns7OJTpANDK0uZrerPGUJPFlOsKGv9TXcOeUXmxLj2tbCSZB1H-xk9NleyF5N5_weNvuXzFdU3aBhAi5NR1GxeOuWmWdYoFbK8OmyxtpLGu_F4xLVCFjDKFMO9MU0K8ymyLHWAyoK8rbVow6OOEaL78nVGjRZ944vrQJw14n7gkyll0qFwywKHVAYR7ZmSWkjBfV4iEcfw/4e3/C_6HjgErSGS9G_eszVbRrg/h3/h001.WLbvr1qpS18_L2FlOaAIFEAceyPF_ZnF7_0XkcNRW78)** You might hear Elon Musk and his crew say this is about cutting costs. Don’t be fooled for a second. **This is all about Trump and Musk breaking things to enrich themselves, even if it means our parks, mountains, and _airplanes_ become more dangerous than ever before.** According to reports, the fired FAA employees were largely responsible for “radar, landing and navigational aid maintenance.” And for _decades_ , USFS and NPS workers have kept our hundreds of national parks accessible, clean, and safe. But Elon Musk doesn’t care about your safety. He doesn’t care about wildfire prevention. **He cares about breaking the entire federal government all so he can steal billions. And every day that he’s in charge, more people are put in danger while his pockets get fuller.** [Please join tens of thousands and sign our petition to Congress today, calling on Musk to be fired. His hijacking and dismantling of government agencies is a threat to all of us.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRImmimh3jp2d-3TZzmeeGfu-UTQdsDuya64KpuO8JCNfpJz62tZbS1uRaEs6xz5o92rDg2XhEAJvsJFbdUoEPfHeyhplR8xlGYyiPdqSrTFjOz2aUIv1O58mTQR6k1Q3SFJRISkVFjG9idUbYtR897heY8VRF7q15AQz_Q5BAo5sOzcece62weiHkB31EO56nnVDauZk70Zl1dZYy-X8dLsYPu8c43ghQphf2t-mpdjUS_aaWW1f8i7R75_Q7E2BBRukair68oiMROmQZzfy1AE4-bB0c4DzG1gnS1K_aJrhC8_V-7A7EHBrNBz2YkqX7dxBK2D2y9hgOgcFFve-0K30eQ6Xo1OGP4aIZsAs8ijYg/4e3/C_6HjgErSGS9G_eszVbRrg/h4/h001.yN3U3ZyBDqpjcNIOc0MSs8jH1q8WiyMu6A0Xg0Kj7s0) Sign the petition, and we’ll follow up with next steps in this fight. In solidarity, Team WFP Sources: 1\. clean_body: | | | Peter, The Musk-Trump rampage to break the federal government has been running full steam ahead. And all of us — across the country — are about to feel the impact. Over the weekend, roughly 3,400 workers in the US Forest Service (USFS) and 1,000 in the National Parks Service (NPS) were terminated1 — many of whom are tasked with wildfire prevention or rescuing stranded visitors. And this morning, several hundred were let go from the Federal Aviation Association 2 — just 18 days after the deadliest commercial plane crash since 2009. These are just a few of the more than 200,000 federal workers in every state who have been targeted for layoffs. And it’s only the beginning. Already signed? to help fuel our organizing. Or take a moment to to be notified as more accountability, rapid-response, and election priority phonebanks are posted. You might hear Elon Musk and his crew say this is about cutting costs. Don’t be fooled for a second. This is all about Trump and Musk breaking things to enrich themselves, even if it means our parks, mountains, and airplanes become more dangerous than ever before. According to reports, the fired FAA employees were largely responsible for “radar, landing and navigational aid maintenance.” And for decades , USFS and NPS workers have kept our hundreds of national parks accessible, clean, and safe. But Elon Musk doesn’t care about your safety. He doesn’t care about wildfire prevention. He cares about breaking the entire federal government all so he can steal billions. And every day that he’s in charge, more people are put in danger while his pockets get fuller. Sign the petition, and we’ll follow up with next steps in this fight. In solidarity, Team WFP Sources: 1\. , The Guardian, February 15, 2025 2\. , Associated Press, February 17, 2025 Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Peter,

**I’m not a career politician. I’m a dairy farmer.**

I know what it’s like to get up for work at 3:30 am, trudge through feet of snow to feed my herd, and make difficult decisions to keep my farm afloat while providing for my family and employees. That’s exactly why I decided to run for Elise Stefanik’s seat and why I’m asking for your support today.

[_**This is the closest House majority ever, so this race presents a real opportunity—and the work we do in the early days of the campaign will play a vital role in our success. Can I count on you to split $25 between my campaign and Laura Gillen to help us prepare for this special election and flip NY-21 blue?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102300033/528726624/849351480?refcode=BLU_EM_LGC_2025-02-17_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NzIwMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNGM2MTczYS03ZWVkLWVmMTEtOTBjYi0wMDIyNDgyYTk0ZjQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HQ-17JknAL7mWopwEg4hGOLQK2vAI8XFoAqzQ1BtfCM=)

# _Featured Video:_ Confronting federal employee protesters about wasting our money on absurd woke BS
PolitiBrawl's Matt Miller **[asked anti-Trump protesters](https://substack.com/redirect/4ab49d3a-04fa-4e0c-ad22-33f581c7beba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , many of whom were federal employees, about the wokest DEI programs funded by USAID with American tax dollars.
"DEI programs are a great use of my tax dollars!" one protester said.
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/4ab49d3a-04fa-4e0c-ad22-33f581c7beba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/4ab49d3a-04fa-4e0c-ad22-33f581c7beba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# Anti-Trump protests have changed a lot...
_By Richie McGinniss_
Walking past the Capitol reflecting pool on my way to the Not My President's Day protest, the crowd numbered in the thousands-far larger than anything I expected given the frigid cold and wind.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/2b417c34-ff55-4243-98e8-1a769ce87cf0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Then again, Presidents' day is a federal holiday, and given the number of federal workers whose jobs were either recently axed or are presently on the chopping block, I guess it should not have come as a surprise to see such a big group. Yet this demonstration was atypical from the countless other "not my president" anti-Trump protests I have documented since 2016.
The first thing I noticed was how much older the average participant was compared to a movement like BLM, where nine out of ten demonstrators were under the age of thirty. In this case more than half were at or above middle age.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/1b2c9f65-0649-49b5-92f0-2a2d5c9bb725?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
As I began asking around, many told me that they were federal workers and didn't want to conduct any interviews on camera. It was also apparent that these protesters had a lot of time on their hands, as evidenced by the abundance of meticulously hand-drawn signs.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8716dfba-9e96-4f1c-9320-e9dc3d829478?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/43e77722-cac8-49a4-8b59-552aad64305c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Another interesting development was the volume of disdain that was directed towards Elon Musk-I saw more Anti-Musk signs and heard more anti-Musk chants than any related to Trump. It seems that DC has grown tired of sounding the Orange Man Bad alarm, and have found their new villain in Musk and his DOGE.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/ce6126d8-3e1e-4e61-a48f-38a8eb375aa6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The notion that Elon Musk is "unelected" and therefore unable to wield power on behalf of Trump carries a unique irony given that unelected bureaucrats at our intelligence agencies have time and again used leaks and infinitely powerful spying technology to undermine Trump over the last nine years (e.g. FISA warranted surveillance of Trump loyalists, Peter Strzok & Lisa Page, 51 Intel officials calling the Hunter Biden Laptop into question, and years long investigation into nonexistent Russian collusion).
**Now a brief word from our sponsor:**
* * *
**[Presented By Goldco](https://substack.com/redirect/776e73a4-49d6-4758-98d8-683180b52bcf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# Gold Breaks Another Record Under President Trump!
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/776e73a4-49d6-4758-98d8-683180b52bcf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The start of the Trump Presidency has been good for gold! The shiny yellow metal has yet again broken another record high in price since Trump has taken the reins, and could go much higher!
It's really no surprise...
Under Trump's first term, the price of gold soared a staggering 53%.
He's even talked about the U.S.A. going back to a gold standard!
"We used to have a very, very solid country because it was based on a gold standard." **\- Donald J. Trump.**
He's also been quoted saying that...
"Bringing back the gold standard would be very hard to do, but boy, would it be wonderful. We'd have a standard on which to base our money." - Donald J. Trump.
Here's the thing: Gold has been used as a store of value for centuries, the government can't easily manipulate it, and it's known as a hedge against market risk and inflation.
**How high will the price of gold go under Trump's second term?**
Will gold continue to break record after record in 2025 and beyond?
Learn more about the benefits of precious metals like gold and silver with a free copy of the Gold & Silver Kit sent directly to your doorstep.
You'll even learn about an IRS Loophole Trump has kept open that allows you to diversify your savings with gold and silver tax and penalty free!
Plus, you could get up to a 10% Instant Match in BONUS SILVER!
So, don't wait a second longer. Get your hands on your free copy of this kit and start your gold and silver journey today!
**[Get Your Free Gold& Silver Kit Today!](https://substack.com/redirect/776e73a4-49d6-4758-98d8-683180b52bcf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Get My Free Kit Today](https://substack.com/redirect/776e73a4-49d6-4758-98d8-683180b52bcf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
As this protest rhetoric evolves, it marks a tectonic shift in the politics of our country. Young people turned out in record numbers to support Trump. Aged employees of institutions like the federal government have been activated by the Trump admin's actions, as was reflected by the demographics of the protest in DC today.
While the chants of "Not my President" were literal during Trump's first term, now they have become metaphorical. Since Trump won the popular vote, his detractors can no longer call him an illegitimate President. It seems that the new enemy is no longer just Trump, but former Democrats-turned cabinet officials like Elon Musk, RFK, and Tulsi Gabbard.
If the best disinfectant is sunlight, then it is imperative Americans continue to engage with never-Trump factions on the merit of their ideas. Check out my interview with this dude in a Guy Fawkes mask for more on how some continue their efforts to censor vigorous debate, while others have become more open with an open dialogue now that Trump has been proven to be more popular than they had hoped. Whether the loudest voices come from the former or the latter categories, as Trump moves into the second month of his second term Americans are becoming more aware of how often they have been lied to, and how pathetic efforts at shutting down the other side become.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/afa94e9c-30f0-452b-abf2-13f7a6beb0f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
What the reader may gather from this is simple: the law fare and nonstop disparagement of Teflon Don didn't work. Now that he is the popularly elected President, the tactics must change. The same, tired tropes that Trump is a Russian agent or a felon have failed. Don't be surprised as those within Trump's orbit become the new bad guys. But as the situation becomes less cut and dry in the eyes of the opposition, and as their media allies continue to erode their credibility in the discourse, that provides an opportunity to challenge these ideas--not trough name calling but through questions and open conversation.
* * *

name: Senator Dick Anderson
subject: Happy President's Day
body:
Senator Anderson Legislative Newsletter
---
| | | [](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fcontent.govdelivery.com%2Faccounts%2FORLEG%2Fbulletins%2F3cfc123/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/-MJnaPwgRzIacnkWW9ZSLrCo7Hb7aghqdeCcs8IncHs=392 "View Online")
---|---
---
|
* * *
---
Welcome back to another newsletter. I hope everyone had a great few weeks and enjoyed the snowy weather we had on the coast!
As **Vice-Chair** for the **Senate Housing Committee** , some of my main responsibilities this session include collaborating with other members to draft legislation, advocating for constituents, working alongside state agencies, local governments and others on housing initiatives and monitoring the progress of housing-related bills ensuring they align with the needs of the state's residents.
Oregon's population continues to grow, and we find ourselves grappling with a pressing issue: **housing**. All types of housing at every income level. From rising costs to a lack of available properties, timelines on building permits, the challenges are only deepened with the complex web of land-use regulations and the growing demand for more housing.
It is clear the current approach to housing in Oregon is not working and has not met the needs of our communities. We have a **deficit of 100,000** homes of what currently is needed in the state. Each month we get further and further from meeting the yearly goal of 36,000 units being built.
**The solutions fall under three things: Land, Regulations, and Infrastructure.**
**LAND:** The availability of land at an affordable price is the main driver of the cost of a home. Costs of buildable land in Urban Growth Boundaries (UGBs) is at an all-time high. However, there are groups in Salem focused on not allowing for more land to build. It is stifling.
**REGULATIONS:** We are working in this area a lot with cities and counties. Some of the areas we are focusing on are timelines on building permits, land use decisions, more inspectors, infill accelerators, and land splitting. We have some good bills moving forward, but regulations are only a minor dollar driver in the cost of a home. However, time is money and the cost of waiting around to be able to build is costing us more in the long run.
**INFRASTRUCTURE:** In 2024 the state was able to get 100 million dollars to cities for their housing projects. This is unsustainable in Oregon; we do not have millions laying around for these costly projects. Water, sewer and roads cost a lot of money—this is a huge driver to the cost of homes. I do not see a path in which the state is going to be able to fund the needed projects across Oregon.
I am committed to exploring common-sense policies that can improve land access, streamline regulations and modernize our infrastructures to ensure every Oregonian has a place to call home. I am welcome to hearing your ideas if you have any!
In this newsletter and future issues, I will dive into key factors of our housing crisis, outline potential policy solutions and highlight the steps we are currently taking to address this issue and many others in Salem head-on.
* * *
---
I hope everyone stayed **warm** during all the **frigid** winter weather we have been having! This is what the **snow storm** last week looked like from my house in **Lincoln City**.
* * *
---
* * *
---
I travelled to Newport to meet with our Federal congressional delegates Senator **Jeff Merkley** and Representative **Val Hoyle**. The bulk of the conversation was centered around federal issues; however, I enjoyed good productive conversation with constituents.
* * *
---
**Go West Oregon Credit Union Day at the Capitol-** This high energy event allows for those working in credit unions to receive advocacy training, gain insight from state leaders and meet with legislators. What a passionate group of people!
* * *
---
I **testified** before the **Joint Transportation Committee** along with Philomath Mayor Chris McMorran in support of my bill [SB 564](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB564/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/HJ7bGGB15Uv5BwVy6dORhtfI8hr3MUMSJSos2YI3IoE=392) which looks into expanding transportation options on a portion of **Highway 20** and **Highway 99** in **Philomath**. This area of road is a true bottleneck between Corvallis and Philomath. The first step in securing funding is to look at all options available and to study the potential effects of those options.
* * *
---
**2025 Oregon Arts and Cultural Caucus Kick-off Even** t- I am a member of the **Arts and Culture Caucus** , the largest bipartisan caucus in the legislature. I had the pleasure of attending the event at the **Elsinore Theater** in Salem. The caucus announced their legislative priorities for the legislative session. There was also a program hosted and curated by jazz artists, educator and **2020 Governor's Arts Award** recipient **Darrell Grant** that featured performers and culture bearers from around the state including**Oregon native** and **Grammy award-winning** artist **Esperanza Spalding**.
* * *
---
# 2025 Committees
* * *
---
* * *
---
**Live from Salem Town Hall- Representative Gomberg** and I are restarting the **ever-popular** town hall sessions. Join us the last **Friday** of each month beginning **February 28th** at **8 AM**!
Look for the link to join from home in my **next newsletter**!
* * *
---
* * *
---
Have you ever wondered how a bill becomes a law? Here's a quick breakdown of the law-making process:
1. **Proposal** : A new law (or amendment) is introduced, typically by a legislator, government official, or through public petition.
2. **Debate** : The proposed bill is reviewed and debated in committees and the legislative chambers (House or Senate), where members discuss, amend, or reject it.
3. **Voting** : If the bill passes through all necessary debates and revisions, it goes for a vote. If both chambers approve it, it moves to the next step.
4. **Approval** : The bill is sent to the executive branch (e.g., President or Governor) for approval. The executive can sign it into law or veto it.
5. **Enactment** : If signed, the bill becomes law and is enforced by the relevant authorities.

* * *
---
Here is an infographic from the **State of Oregon Legislature** on the step-by-step process for a bill to become a law. If you would like to know more, please click [HERE.](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fcitizen_engagement%2FPages%2FHow-an-Idea-Becomes-Law.aspx/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/Dm_2nzIk8Y1KhkBGmiR9ouMixBCgE326zSKt2d5DEDc=392)
* * *
---
[](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv=Otbml6WIQPo/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/DbsVh14BHgZy27Li1apvUueNk9uHHKisuNftNMJGX-M=392 "School House Rock- How a bill becomes a law")
---
**School House Rock** \- "🎶 'I'm just a bill, yes I'm only a bill...' 🎶 From idea to law, this classic Schoolhouse Rock video shows how a bill makes its way through Congress! Watch and learn how laws are made the fun way!
* * *
---
[](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv=66f4-NKEYz4/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/OvTL23CwAQI9LNqZqlHZGCziZBbZ9VrfNmIK-BT-HdA=392 "Crash Course- How a bill becomes a law")
---
**Crash Course: How a Bill Becomes a Law** \- 💡Ever wondered how a bill becomes a law? This **Crash Course** video breaks it down step by step! 🏛️ From introduction to approval, get the full breakdown of the legislative process in a quick, easy-to-understand way.📜
* * *
---
* * *
---
There are so many bills this legislative session **(Over 3000)** that it might be overwhelming to know which bills to follow. Here are a few that I am a **Chief-Sponsor** of that are going through the legislative process now.
[SB 896-](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB896/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/jsKyu935PRHQJNAe5vZO4DYkcq_yRosG-IJP9sXVZs0=392) Creates a grant program to be used to fund after-school learning activities.
[SB 564](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB564/2/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/QcafzGiP_ok_6Sm0aZfp5OWcUmB0pXtiieAlRUm8PFk=392)\- The Act tells ODOT to widen the part of U.S. Highway 20 between Highway 99 and the city of Philomath.
[SB 439](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB439/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/nVJg_x41jSjs-cCJ3IoboZUoDUb7XISnhm5meuxULdM=392)\- Says that building a childcare center in a place that provides affordable housing does not mean that a person building the whole place must pay prevailing wage. But building the childcare center itself does require paying prevailing wage for labor.
[SB 405](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1%2FMeasures%2FOverview%2FSB405/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/DaonQBPmz77jKRLzv8b2cRC4700du_wP9Bz6Zv33Mlk=392)\- The Act would match the estate tax exemption to the federal amount. Provides that, for the estates of decedents dying on or after January 1, 2026, Oregon estate tax is not due unless the value of the Oregon taxable estate exceeds $13.61 million. Raises the threshold for the filing of an estate tax return to a gross estate valued at more than $13.61 million.
* * *
---
📉 **New jobs report** : Since Jan. 2020, Oregon added just **1,392 private-sector jobs** , while **government jobs grew by 17,583**. Not surprising—Oregon ranks **48th in business friendliness**. Our policies should attract businesses & jobs, not drive them away.
* * *
---
**Oregon Treasury Unclaimed Property Day-** Did you know 1 in 7 **Oregonians** have unclaimed property? **February 1** marked National Unclaimed Property Day, a perfect opportunity to check if any of Oregon’s more than**$1 billion** in unclaimed property belongs to you or someone you know. The **Oregon State Treasury** runs the statewide unclaimed property program, safeguarding and reconnecting Oregonians with forgotten assets like **uncashed checks** , **forgotten bank accounts** or **insurance payouts** , or even the **contents of safe deposit boxes**.
It’s easy to search and free to claim. Visit [unclaimed.oregon.gov](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Funclaimed.oregon.gov%2F/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/OSdpR2bOQUVL7ekIma70VtFo8NmE531iI3mDnKcWe3g=392) to see if there’s money waiting for you! Don’t miss this chance to reconnect with your **lost assets** —it could be as simple as typing in your name. Share this important message with your community and help spread the word about **reclaiming** what’s rightfully yours.
* * *
---
**January 26th- February 1st** was **National School Choice Wee** k. I believe that all Oregon students deserve access to a high-quality education, regardless of zip code they live in.
* * *
---
| | I spoke on the**Senate Floor** relating to school choice and how **Charter Schools in Oregon** only receive **48 cents on the dollar** compared to **other public schools** in the state. **Charter schools** are also outperforming other schools in**English Language Arts and Math**. I believe that we can and should do better to support our public charter schools and school choice in the state. Click [HERE](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fyoutube.com%2Fshorts%2FrhPIahtZw14%3Ffeature=share/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/-HYBdTc6OOynSSNzjYI-yaOxkzLlKX9p2pDRV87VAMw=392) to watch my senate floor remonstrance.
---|---|---
* * *
---
**Happy Birthday Oregon!** Join me in celebrating **Oregon's birthday** , a day to honor the rich history and culture of our beloved state! On February 14th, we commemorate the official day **Oregon** became the **33rd state in the Union in 1859**. It's a perfect time to reflect on the **natural beauty** ,**diverse communities** , and the **pioneering spirit** that make **Oregon** truly unique.
* * *
---
As we celebrate **Presidents' Day** this year, take a moment to **reflect** on the **leadership** that has shaped our nation. This**holiday** is not just a **tribute to past presidents** , but a reminder of the **values** that bind us as a country—**unity, integrity, and perseverance**.
**Presidents' Day** offers an opportunity to appreciate the vision of those who **led our country** through both times of**prosperity** and **adversity**. It’s also a great moment to think about the **impact** of**leadership** in our daily lives, whether in our **communities** , our **businesses** , or our **personal goals**.
* * *

# 83rd Legislative Session Senate Honorary Page Program
* * *
---
**Senate Honorary Page Program** \- The page program provides students with a unique opportunity to **engage with the legislative process** by assisted with **Senate Chamber** tasks, learning about the role of **legislature** , and observing the work of the Senate**firsthand**.
This **long-cherished** program is a valuable way for students to gain**civic education** and **connect** with the **legislative process** in a **meaningful** and **memorable** way. We believe the opportunity serves as an **entry point** for our **future leaders** , directly **engaging** them in the process.
If you know a student in the district that is interested in applying, they can learn more about the program [HERE](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fsecretary-of-senate%2FPages%2Fhonorary-page-program.aspx/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/9g1W00a-pAJAllLETQ9W8AkvimJVGpyeiM4ZmnPbEWw=392) and **fill out an application** for the program [HERE](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fsecretary-of-senate%2FDocuments%2F2025%2520Honorary%2520Page%2520Application.pdf/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/KkS1iDxUutWyYYGseYLI2UWvuGbXYUTTjSj7PfLhtIs=392).
If you have any questions, please reach out to **Misty McCord** at **Misty.McCord@oregonlegislature.gov**.
* * *
---
* * *
---
**Bay Area Hospital** \- I spoke with journalist **Nigel Jaquiss** from **Willamette Weekly** relating to the Bay Area Hospital in **Coos Bay** and how I have encouraged both sides to work together to save the hospital.
Read the article [HERE](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.wweek.com%2Fnews%2F2025%2F02%2F05%2Fworkers-at-coasts-biggest-hospital-mobilize-to-stave-off-private-equity-firm%2F/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/dV6ZnMsemA9wT6dhnpTiAeX7LIJWwQjwa_H1dy6FO0c=392)
* * *
---
**Schooner Creek Discovery Park** \- The new **Schooner Creek Discovery Park** is in Phase 2 of the 3 phased park project **$11 million park project**. **Phase 1** construction broke ground this past summer with a grand opening in **December** of the first inclusive and accessible public playground on the **Oregon Coas** t. This playground is designed to promote **inclusive play** , which offers people of differing abilities and all ages the same play.**Phase 2** design is in process for a special event stage, lawn area, and entry plaza. Construction is planned for completion in the Fall of 2025. **Phase 3** will start in the Fall of 2025, with construction planned for completion by **Fall 2026**. **Phase 3** amenities include a covered multi-sports court, permanent pickleball courts, turfed multi-sports field, and walking paths. **Lincoln City** would like to express their appreciation for the $1.0 million that the Oregon State Legislature has funded for this project.
Learn more about **Schooner Creek Discovery Park** [HERE.](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.lincolncityparksandrec.org%2Fschooner-creek-discovery-park/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/96OqRVl7_JeXF47vhOQk9LH4awYu1j3RvHqHKfbcPMI=392)
* * *
---
* * *
---
_Pictured Left to Right:**Erin Reynolds** , **Senator Anderson** , and Florence Mayor **Rob Ward**. _
* * *
---
Our first district spotlight is the **City of Florence**. My office manager Sam, met with **Erin Renyolds** and **Megan Maxwell** to discuss some of their favorite things about the city they live and work in. Centrally located in the district, **Florence** is a municipality that serves about **9,400 residents** who live within city limits and about **9,000** more who live outside of city limits.
Florence transitioned from the **Timber and Fishing industries** a while ago, and the city is a hub for tourism of all types. The **Central Oregon Coastal** area is a great place to **hike, camp, paddleboard, ATV** and **off road** , **explore** and **eat** some great food at some of the local restaurants and explore the**Old Town** area of the city.
The **Old Town** area of **Florence, Oregon** , is **charming and full of character**. Nestled along the beautiful **Oregon Coast** , it offers a blend of historic buildings, quaint shops, and scenic views. You can stroll down streets lined with unique boutiques, art galleries, cafes, and restaurants, many of which focus on local products and the region's coastal vibe. The area has a**cozy** , **small-town feel** , with architecture that reflects the town's **maritime history**.
**Old Town** also sits right next to the **Siuslaw River** , with views of the historic **Siuslaw River Bridge** , an iconic part of the town's skyline. The **picturesque** **riverfront** adds to the relaxing atmosphere, and visitors can often enjoy walking the docks or simply taking in the peaceful surroundings. Overall, **Old Town Florence** is a mix of history, nature and local flavor- a great spot to explore if you're in the area!
The **Florence Event Center** is also a part of the cities long list of amenities that are offered, it is really a focal point of the community. The 21,000 square foot building has tons of space for multiple meetings, banquets and conferences. There's a 455-seat theater that can house **Broadway-style theater** productions. The local community uses the **FEC** for elementary school performances, talent shows, PTA fundraisers, band and orchestra performances, and many other community events. The Florence Event Center is an elevated event space that brings in out of area tourism to the area by housing music festivals, conventions and even weddings!
* * *
---
---
The next event at the **Florence Event Center** is the **27th Home & Garden Show** **February 28th** from 12 PM-6 PM and **March 1st** from 10 AM-6PM. If you are in the area, be sure and stop by!
Learn more about the **Home & Garden Show** [HERE.](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.eventcenter.org%2Fgeneral%2Fpage%2Fhome-garden-show-0/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/vnVRuJarnxgJOtBKTuk3x4AzMXYFUJsfJENNFQ6MqL8=392)

* [OLIS- Oregon Legislative Information System](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Folis.oregonlegislature.gov%2Fliz%2F2025R1/1/010101951611ba21-045fcd12-805d-43ed-b729-27a7e796afe7-000000/U1EtoFy1Z9LaV8vxtpgxdGXyiP8TMz32PDyn9itsxKs=392)\- This is where you can find extensive details about every bill as well as follow them through the legislative process. This is also where you can view committee hearings online.

Links to find your legislatures, how to follow bills and watch meetings, how to testify and visiting the Capitol.

clean_body: Senator Anderson Legislative Newsletter | | | "View Online" ---|--- | Welcome back to another newsletter. I hope everyone had a great few weeks and enjoyed the snowy weather we had on the coast! As Vice-Chair for the Senate Housing Committee , some of my main responsibilities this session include collaborating with other members to draft legislation, advocating for constituents, working alongside state agencies, local governments and others on housing initiatives and monitoring the progress of housing-related bills ensuring they align with the needs of the state's residents. Oregon's population continues to grow, and we find ourselves grappling with a pressing issue: housing. All types of housing at every income level. From rising costs to a lack of available properties, timelines on building permits, the challenges are only deepened with the complex web of land-use regulations and the growing demand for more housing. It is clear the current approach to housing in Oregon is not working and has not met the needs of our communities. We have a deficit of 100,000 homes of what currently is needed in the state. Each month we get further and further from meeting the yearly goal of 36,000 units being built. The solutions fall under three things: Land, Regulations, and Infrastructure. LAND: The availability of land at an affordable price is the main driver of the cost of a home. Costs of buildable land in Urban Growth Boundaries (UGBs) is at an all-time high. However, there are groups in Salem focused on not allowing for more land to build. It is stifling. REGULATIONS: We are working in this area a lot with cities and counties. Some of the areas we are focusing on are timelines on building permits, land use decisions, more inspectors, infill accelerators, and land splitting. We have some good bills moving forward, but regulations are only a minor dollar driver in the cost of a home. However, time is money and the cost of waiting around to be able to build is costing us more in the long run. INFRASTRUCTURE: In 2024 the state was able to get 100 million dollars to cities for their housing projects. This is unsustainable in Oregon; we do not have millions laying around for these costly projects. Water, sewer and roads cost a lot of money—this is a huge driver to the cost of homes. I do not see a path in which the state is going to be able to fund the needed projects across Oregon. I am committed to exploring common-sense policies that can improve land access, streamline regulations and modernize our infrastructures to ensure every Oregonian has a place to call home. I am welcome to hearing your ideas if you have any! In this newsletter and future issues, I will dive into key factors of our housing crisis, outline potential policy solutions and highlight the steps we are currently taking to address this issue and many others in Salem head-on. I hope everyone stayed warm during all the frigid winter weather we have been having! This is what the snow storm last week looked like from my house in Lincoln City. I travelled to Newport to meet with our Federal congressional delegates Senator Jeff Merkley and Representative Val Hoyle. The bulk of the conversation was centered around federal issues; however, I enjoyed good productive conversation with constituents. Go West Oregon Credit Union Day at the Capitol- This high energy event allows for those working in credit unions to receive advocacy training, gain insight from state leaders and meet with legislators. What a passionate group of people! I testified before the Joint Transportation Committee along with Philomath Mayor Chris McMorran in support of my bill which looks into expanding transportation options on a portion of Highway 20 and Highway 99 in Philomath. This area of road is a true bottleneck between Corvallis and Philomath. The first step in securing funding is to look at all options available and to study the potential effects of those options. 2025 Oregon Arts and Cultural Caucus Kick-off Even t- I am a member of the Arts and Culture Caucus , the largest bipartisan caucus in the legislature. I had the pleasure of attending the event at the Elsinore Theater in Salem. The caucus announced their legislative priorities for the legislative session. There was also a program hosted and curated by jazz artists, educator and 2020 Governor's Arts Award recipient Darrell Grant that featured performers and culture bearers from around the state includingOregon native and Grammy award-winning artist Esperanza Spalding. 2025 Committees Live from Salem Town Hall- Representative Gomberg and I are restarting the ever-popular town hall sessions. Join us the last Friday of each month beginning February 28th at 8 AM! Look for the link to join from home in my next newsletter! Have you ever wondered how a bill becomes a law? Here's a quick breakdown of the law-making process: 1. Proposal : A new law (or amendment) is introduced, typically by a legislator, government official, or through public petition. 2. Debate : The proposed bill is reviewed and debated in committees and the legislative chambers (House or Senate), where members discuss, amend, or reject it. 3. Voting : If the bill passes through all necessary debates and revisions, it goes for a vote. If both chambers approve it, it moves to the next step. 4. Approval : The bill is sent to the executive branch (e.g., President or Governor) for approval. The executive can sign it into law or veto it. 5. Enactment : If signed, the bill becomes law and is enforced by the relevant authorities. Here is an infographic from the State of Oregon Legislature on the step-by-step process for a bill to become a law. If you would like to know more, please click "School House Rock- How a bill becomes a law" School House Rock \- "🎶 'I'm just a bill, yes I'm only a bill...' 🎶 From idea to law, this classic Schoolhouse Rock video shows how a bill makes its way through Congress! Watch and learn how laws are made the fun way! "Crash Course- How a bill becomes a law" Crash Course: How a Bill Becomes a Law \- 💡Ever wondered how a bill becomes a law? This Crash Course video breaks it down step by step! 🏛️ From introduction to approval, get the full breakdown of the legislative process in a quick, easy-to-understand way.📜 There are so many bills this legislative session (Over 3000) that it might be overwhelming to know which bills to follow. Here are a few that I am a Chief-Sponsor of that are going through the legislative process now. Creates a grant program to be used to fund after-school learning activities. \- The Act tells ODOT to widen the part of U.S. Highway 20 between Highway 99 and the city of Philomath. \- Says that building a childcare center in a place that provides affordable housing does not mean that a person building the whole place must pay prevailing wage. But building the childcare center itself does require paying prevailing wage for labor. \- The Act would match the estate tax exemption to the federal amount. Provides that, for the estates of decedents dying on or after January 1, 2026, Oregon estate tax is not due unless the value of the Oregon taxable estate exceeds $13.61 million. Raises the threshold for the filing of an estate tax return to a gross estate valued at more than $13.61 million. 📉 New jobs report : Since Jan. 2020, Oregon added just 1,392 private-sector jobs , while government jobs grew by 17,583. Not surprising—Oregon ranks 48th in business friendliness. Our policies should attract businesses & jobs, not drive them away. February 1 marked National Unclaimed Property Day, a perfect opportunity to check if any of Oregon’s more than$1 billion in unclaimed property belongs to you or someone you know. The Oregon State Treasury runs the statewide unclaimed property program, safeguarding and reconnecting Oregonians with forgotten assets like uncashed checks , forgotten bank accounts or insurance payouts , or even the contents of safe deposit boxes. It’s easy to search and free to claim. Visit to see if there’s money waiting for you! Don’t miss this chance to reconnect with your lost assets —it could be as simple as typing in your name. Share this important message with your community and help spread the word about reclaiming what’s rightfully yours. January 26th- February 1st was National School Choice Wee k. The local community uses the FEC for elementary school performances, talent shows, PTA fundraisers, band and orchestra performances, and many other community events. The Florence Event Center is an elevated event space that brings in out of area tourism to the area by housing music festivals, conventions and even weddings! The next event at the Florence Event Center is the 27th Home & Garden Show February 28th from 12 PM-6 PM and March 1st from 10 AM-6PM. If you are in the area, be sure and stop by! Learn more about the Home & Garden Show If you know anyone in our district that you believe should be nominated for our next District Spotlight, please email my Office Manager Sam at Samantha.Bebout@oregonlegislature.gov with your nominations! | | Bryan Iverson Chief of Staff Bryan.Iverson@oregonlegislature.gov ---|---|--- Michael Town II Legislative Director Michael.Town@oregonlegislature.gov | | ---|---|--- | | Samantha Bebout Office Manager Samantha.Bebout@oregonlegislature.gov ---|---|--- The connection between people and their governmen t is bolstered when the public has substantial opportunity to let their concerns be heard by their elected officials. Below are a few ways that YOU can join in on thelegislative process. \- This is where you can find extensive details about every bill as well as follow them through the legislative process. This is also where you can view committee hearings online. \- This is a wonderful resource with everything you will need to know about how to participate in the Legislative Session. Links to find your legislatures, how to follow bills and watch meetings, how to testify and visiting the Capitol. Capitol Phone: 503-986-1705 Capitol Address: 900 Court St NE, S-303, Salem, OR, 97301 Email:mailto:Sen.DickAnderson@oregonlegislature.gov Website: Replies to this message are sent to an unmonitored mailbox. To contact me, please click here: mailto:Sen.DickAnderson@oregonlegislature.gov SUBSCRIBER SERVICES OPTIONS: • To stop receiving this newsletter, add new newsletters, ordelete your subscription services click • For questions about subscriber services, please email Information provided may not be final and is subject to change.

name: Oregon GOP
subject: Honoring Our Nation’s Presidents
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE OREGON REPUBLICAN PARTY (COMMITTEE ID 379) 187 HIGH ST
body:
A Day to Reflect on the Leadership that Shaped America

| | | | [ ](https://oregon.gop/)
---
| | Friend, This Presidents' Day, we take a moment to reflect on the incredible leaders who have shaped the history of our nation.
---
| | [ ](https://links.infooregongop.com/els/v2/q9bXt9bDw4Hq/RFJTd2grTVdnaUZBZFVSNXpsR2tBdlBYUUljcGwvNTJzUktxb2NnQU81VUlvejQ5b1VEY0llNFVUaElucUZNWmJUZ3BVWjNidXE2UnlpSWhZbEpWUTg4d0NPL2puVTE5ZndaNGo3dEZWU2c9S0/)
---
| | From George Washington’s courageous leadership in the American Revolution to Ronald Reagan’s dedication to restoring America's strength on the world stage, Presidents' Day serves as a reminder of the remarkable individuals who have served our country with honor and vision. Throughout history, our presidents have faced great challenges, from wars to economic crises, yet they have striven to uphold the values that make America exceptional: **freedom, opportunity, and industry, with a government accountable to the people.** These leaders, through their actions and policies, have played a key role in forging the nation we know today. Presidents' Day is not just a day for reflection but also a chance to recognize the ongoing importance of leadership in shaping the future of our nation. As we honor our past presidents, let us also remember the importance of our civic duty and the role we play in the future of our country. Thank you for your continued commitment to the values we all hold dear. Oregon GOP
---
---

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/102300492/528728421/1096283482?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS8xMDE4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTEwNmJkZjMtODJlZC1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5NGY0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=10I8-vc61Ehtnabj1P5MTk15x0BMUZFSJ2weXjA1Wsk=&emci=ad125954-78ed-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=9106bdf3-82ed-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&ceid=16746905)

Hi Friend,
So, Trump’s outrageous attempt to fire all the independent watchdogs at federal agencies dealing with corruption, ethics, and misuse of taxpayer money is going to the Supreme Court.

Yes! The radical right-wing MAGA majority SCOTUS beholden to Trump for appointing them to their lifetime jobs with no binding ethics rules or accountability.

Who do you think they’re going to side with?

This is Alan Cohn from Term Limit the Court, the national campaign working with members of Congress, including Senator Adam Schiff, Leader Hakeem Jeffries, and a total of 75 members of the House and Senate, to adopt 18-year term limits.

This kind of unbridled and illegal overreach is made possible because right now, there isn’t a single check on the power of Trump.

It’s time to change that!

We created a movement just over a year ago fueled by Trump’s crime wave, the robbing of the rights of women to make their own healthcare decisions, and the realization of 70% of Americans that no one should have a job for life.

Here’s our plan and how and why we need your help. We’re raising what’s needed to back candidates in the House and Senate pledging to back the TERM Act and dip into that 70% of Americans who agree to mount a major public pressure campaign.

Given everything happening today and what is yet to come, we’d be grateful for your help!

Term Limit the Court

The American people have lost confidence in the Supreme Court after a series of rulings that strike at the core of our freedoms. _There are term limits for a President. Term limits for many governors and state legislators. And the time has come for term limits for members of the Supreme Court._

| Peter,

After spending millions to get Trump elected, Elon Musk has secured a powerful role in the White House – heading the newly created Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).

Its purpose? Shrinking the government and cutting spending for programs and grants that millions of Americans rely on to pay for Trump’s massive tax cuts for corporations and his ultra-wealthy buddies.

**Emerge women are on the front lines against the Musk government takeover!** In Congress, Emerge alums like **Rep. LaMonica McIver** (NJ-10) are stepping up and speaking out to fight back and defend their communities from the chaos coming out of the Trump administration.

clean_body: Emerge women are on the front lines against the Musk government takeover! | = | Peter, After spending millions to get Trump elected, Elon Musk has secured a powerful role in the White House – heading the newly created Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). Its purpose? Shrinking the government and cutting spending for programs and grants that millions of Americans rely on to pay for Trump’s massive tax cuts for corporations and his ultra-wealthy buddies. Emerge women are on the front lines against the Musk government takeover! In Congress, Emerge alums like Rep. LaMonica McIver (NJ-10) are stepping up and speaking out to fight back and defend their communities from the chaos coming out of the Trump administration. = | = DOGE now has access to the Treasury Department’s payment systems and sensitive information of the American people. An unelected billionaire should NOT be able to buy his way into the White House, control government funding, and jeopardize the financial security of everyday people! = | | = | = | = | | = | = | = | = Let’s get to work! Team Emerge * | | = Paid for by Emerge America Emerge America 1001 Connecticut Ave NW Washington, DC 20036 United States

#
Dear Friend of Life,

Did you know Planned Parenthood receives nearly $700 million in taxpayer funding every year--while ending 392,715 preborn lives last year alone?

**The killing must stop.**

For the first time in years, we have a real chance to defund Planned Parenthood--our national leaders need to hear from you.

Elon Musk has pledged to eliminate government waste and corruption at the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE)--and he has signaled that Planned Parenthood should not receive taxpayer money.

**But we need to ensure they follow through.**

Will you sign the petition today to tell President Trump, RFK, Jr., and Musk: No more taxpayer-funded murder of America's sons and daughters!

**Use this secure form:** **[https://www.liveaction.org/end-taxpayer-dollars-to-planned-parenthood/](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/fGORt-JR6OO99crIglJGSNcydshK3LzlB3VW1WJB092jY2NEGM9OkvH8H1PZPOQyK1jsbKRIMXdAS4Zjb3GDjpj8ZU8tiB7VuvCoMrHTuxJZN_tEdtjEx-bqRv9mEMJHgq_2EkSLsJpdzRTqPDbNKJKoJ4C_EM2Kiv0I97ebwBqEvx19_zrz34OqFIZSl9HqBAT_ziyYzzOzKT3z2_Y4-0G73HWZWtkxy231Oo4sqGFl_Q0vRsRo2grNQDICjJTzNMtWdE2T-e-P-sMftVDQ4wKejGKDAP1-TRMXIzk486Dmqw23NgKH82pRYCqh-rxjCc22La_E-JAgUfSLjmBqwJxFki6U2ZXAub2j3MewD9jaJFEKdJdZR2T0u8YNCNav2IZ_A8otrMvrx0WLsyVoGdyOrgiPKRC9EOceR1ojacdNBad7UyJyJxUMghs6/LR5O5GnXvnCTVJ0YRn_kJpCO8kNKugwN/6)**

We can protect preborn lives, end taxpayer-funded abortion, and reverse cultural decline--but only if we take action now. Will you stand with us?

Josef Lipp
**Chief Operating Officer**

name: Coach Tuberville
subject: Allow me to introduce myself.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TUBERVILLE VICTORY FUND
body:
As a successful College Football coach, I want you to understand why I entered into politics
| [ ](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=f60dd553a4bf4860a9ce1f4b130d013f&_e=sIN2PpaNvhjLYxhRWZyD8_MmafFTiWnIgszYqlpHBGhWzvn-s93b3LAIBxZ0k-ODOTOy4oUc0CSTFp-3AhH0n3KCfl_xaencgD_iK2Iw6rEdkb674qdQPUDMfVwp5rGcDvAEgM8hItuN8Hm6Yk-n_IrvWailzluHl0SYjv2GHQKL9wSH0r51peMNG8SlA9e9-Y6Yaqm9AcOjgdhCU4p1kQLnOkSNycvFESKEHPoy6-Rc1Nxli2jDMLk9w8E__mUytjtLHv3N1L8MhNe_srVBUdbgpSy-n9dANVPnOyEiWSzxwzzVpDMSQ0OmcKABT5go9ciF_P3JLF2E53h_T6J3gw%3D%3D)
---

Peter,

It's me, Tommy Tuberville.

Throughout my life, I've been called many names. Whether it's Coach, Dad, Husband, or Senator, I've always led by example for the people who depend on me.

Meet Coach Tuberville:

**Coach Tuberville was born in Camden, Arkansas, the third of three children of Olive and Charles Tuberville. College Football 's 2004 Coach of the Year and two-time SEC Coach of the Year, Tommy Tuberville is most often recognized for his success on the field. But it is the legacy of his father, a highly decorated WWII veteran and Purple Heart recipient, that motivates Coach Tuberville to give back to the brave men and women of the United States Armed Forces.

In 1989 Coach Tuberville met his future wife, Suzanne Fette, at the Sugar Bowl in New Orleans. They were married in 1991 and have two boys, Tucker and Troy, who continue to make their dad proud.

In his career as a College Football Coach, Tommy started a long and successful career at the University of Miami, starting as a graduate assistant and rising to the Hurricanes' defensive coordinator. He led the Canes to three AP national championships and coached future Hall of Famers Ray Lewis and Warren Sapp, as well as Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson.

In 1998, Coach Tuberville was announced as the next head coach for the Auburn Tigers. Tuberville's time at Auburn was a success by any measure, as the Tigers captured eight consecutive bowl appearances, including five New Year's Day bowl appearances, five SEC Western Division titles, one SEC Championship, and a 13-0 season in 2004, after which Coach Tuberville was once again named the AP's SEC Coach of the Year. For his efforts during the perfect 2004 season, Tuberville would be named Paul "Bear" Bryant Coach of the Year, Associated Press Coach of the Year, Walter Camp Coach of the Year, Sporting News Coach of the Year, and AFCA Coach of the Year, among others.

_In 2020, Coach Tuberville became Senator Tuberville, winning his election to represent all Alabamians in the United States Senate with the full support of President Trump. Since then, he's supported President Trump's America-First policies while fighting off the Radical Left. In his first term as Senator, Tommy was one of eight Republicans to vote against certifying the 2020 election, has been a strong pro-life advocate, and called out Democrats for being pro-crime.

Now, Coach Tuberville is running for re-election so he can continue representing Alabamians and Americans in the United States Senate. The Radical Left has him circled as a prime target to defeat in the 2026 Midterm Elections. Can he count on your support? _

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: The Week Ahead, Tuesday February 18th
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

Action is the key to victory and the antidote to despair. Here are the
challenges and the responses that allow us to do what we can with what we
have where we are:

Constitutional crisis in our nation’s Capitol. Donald Trump, Elon Musk,
their enablers and the quislings who accommodate and aid their assault on
the constitution and the rule of law must be stopped. Last week we learned
that, in addition to illegally impounding Congressionally appropriated
funds and dismantling Congressionally chartered agencies they are
illegally forcing federal prosecutors to suspend corruption charges
against those who have betrayed the public trust (e.g. NYC Mayor Eric
Adams).

Then, this weekend, Donald Trump tweeted “He who saves his Country does
not violate any Law”. He now not only claims that he is above the law, but
also clearly encourages his supporters that they can support Trump,
potentially violently, with complete impunity. Remember, this man who
encouraged violence in his first term that led to the murder of 23 people
in El Paso in 2019.

I’m asking you to call your representative; I’m asking you to call your
Senator. And I’m asking you to tell them to fight every single thing that
Donald Trump wants to do. It doesn’t matter how big it is, it doesn’t
matter how small. There is a time for compromise and consensus. THIS IS
NOT THAT TIME. This is a government that is openly promising to end
American democracy and the rule of law. You have all of the proof you
need. It is not theoretical, it is not possible, it is happening right
now.

U.S. Capitol switchboard: 202-224-3121

School voucher fight in Texas. After the Texas Senate passed a $1 billion
school voucher bill earlier this month, all eyes are on the Texas House
which will either ratify the Senate’s action, modify it or defeat the
proposal entirely. The Texas voucher plan will drain hundreds of millions
of public tax dollars from public schools while giving families whose
children already go to private school public money to do so.

This will utterly screw rural Texans (where there are no private schools
to send your kid to), it will screw resource-starved urban districts (who
are already struggling to make ends meet on the meager allotment they
currently receive) and it will screw every Texas homeowner and renter as
districts across the state are forced to raise property taxes to cover the
expected shortfall.

This whole scheme is being funded by rightwing Christian nationalists who
have given Governor Abbott tens of millions of dollars to elect
pro-voucher stooges to the legislature.

Two things you can do:

1) Find out what it will do to your local school district and then share
the news with friends and family (directly, through email or on social
media). [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/64231?t=1001&akid=239986%2E444211%2EoZGj9p ]dontdefundmyschool.com

2) Call your State Representative and tell them to vote NO on vouchers:
512-463-4630

Reach, register and mobilize voters in Texas. Sign up to become a Powered
By People volunteer if you haven’t already. We believe in person-to-person
organizing — meaning, human physical presence where young and
low-propensity voters are. Nothing against digital organizing, phone
banking and social media. But that’s not how we’re going to get our
country back. People connecting with people to overcome injustice and
authoritarianism and win critical elections. It is not too soon to begin
the work for the 2026, 2028 or 2030 elections. In fact, it is absolutely
necessary that we start now.

Here is the link: [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1002&akid=239986%2E444211%2EoZGj9p ]poweredxpeople.org

Above all else, we must keep the faith and keep up the fight. That means
keeping despair at bay, actively pursuing hope and optimism and backing
that up with the action that will allow us to fulfill its promise.

Beto

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=239986%2E444211%2EoZGj9p

PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE

name: Official Democratic Headquarters
body:

$3. That’s the contribution we're asking you to make to the DNC today. $3 because Donald Trump and Elon Musk are wreaking havoc on our democracy and on people’s lives, and this moment demands a strong Party that can organize and mobilize against them. $3 because it’s clearer than ever that we need to elect Democrats at all levels of government who will check Trump’s power in their cities, districts, and states. $3 because the DNC is the organization best positioned to move urgent resources to the places they’re needed most as we take on the fights ahead. [**Please use this link to contribute $3 to the DNC today:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-february-2025?amount=3&refcode=em_20250217_2797_fr&refcode2=2797_22930333_UBSzZT&akid=2797%2E22930333%2EUBSzZT) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_ | | | **[CONTRIBUTE $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-february-2025?express_lane=true&amount=3&refcode=em_20250217_2797_fr&refcode2=2797_22930333_UBSzZT&akid=2797%2E22930333%2EUBSzZT)**
